title: Cortexel skill contract source (meta-schema)
description:
Every file in contract/skills/ must satisfy this. It is what makes 'one authority' enforceable rather than aspirational: a skill's scientific meaning, request schema, validators, budgets, disclosures, accessibility, evidence, ownership, and migration all live in ONE reviewable document, and every generated artifact is derived from it.

Free-form executable expressions are forbidden anywhere in a contract source. A semantic rule is a NAMED validator id with parameters — never a string of code to evaluate.

id.description: The catalog id. Simulator-neutral: it names what the figure MEANS, not which simulator produced the data or which technology draws it.

revision.description: Bumped whenever this contract's accepted meaning or output changes.

status.description: Closed. There is no `beta`, no `preview`, and no free text: a status is a promise, and an ambiguous promise is a broken one.

releaseReady.description: Whether this contract's evidence is actually complete. Kept SEPARATE from `status` so a label can never substitute for evidence.

canonicalQuestion.description: The exact scientific question this figure can answer.

cannotEstablish.description: What it CANNOT establish. Required, and required to be non-empty: a contract that lists no limits is not a scientific contract.

science.description: The reviewable scientific specification. Prose here is normative: it is what an independent reviewer reads BEFORE looking at any implementation.

normalization.description: Accepted order, stable sorting, duplicate policy, missing values, signed zero, unit conversion, interval convention, category ordering.

derivation.description: Each algorithm, its formula, denominators, bins, edge handling, and complexity.

uncertaintySupport.description: Exactly which UncertaintyV1 variants this figure can render. Anything else is refused rather than approximated.

requestSchema.description: The Draft 2020-12 schemas for `data` and `parameters`. Both MUST set additionalProperties:false — a mistyped scientific field has to fail closed, not be silently ignored.

semanticValidators.description: Named cross-field rules from contract/registries/semantic-validators.v1.json. JSON Schema checks SHAPE; these check MEANING.

renderer.description: The render-plan compiler that owns this figure end to end. A stable skill MUST have one: `scene: null` is not a renderer, it is an absence of one.

compactionPolicies.description: Which compaction policies are scientifically VALID for this figure. An empty list means the figure refuses to compact — for a distribution, extrema sampling would destroy the mass, so refusing is the only honest option.

requiredSourceFields.description: Source-declaration fields this figure cannot be interpreted without.

disclosures.description: Disclosure rule ids this figure may emit. Each is triggered by a machine-checkable artifact fact, never by caller text.

summaryTemplate.description: A deterministic template. It states the quantity, unit, domain, counts, method, and limitations. It contains no interpretive claim such as `significant`, `increased`, or `caused`, and it is never generated by a language model at render time.

tableColumns.description: The exact-value alternative. Required for every stable figure: a chart nobody can read the numbers out of is not accessible.

handVectors.description: Whether hand-computable microfixtures exist. MANDATORY for every stable formula — two libraries can share a convention error, but arithmetic you can do on paper cannot.

legacyIds.description: Pre-1.0 ids that migrate HERE. These are accepted only by `cortexel migrate`, never by normal validation: a silent alias makes a stored artifact ambiguous about what was actually validated.

owner.description: An accountable person. A team name with no person behind it is not ownership.

changePolicy.description: Which fields, defaults, algorithms, or output facts would make a change MAJOR.

valid.description: Living fixtures. The test suite asserts these validate, so they cannot rot.

invalid.description: The NEAREST misuse for each rule, with the exact code it must produce. A rule with no invalid vector is not actually enforced.
